_comment:
First composite recipe in the catalogue — sequential calibration sweep (compass → accelerometer → gyro) followed by an arm probe to verify the vehicle is actually arm-ready before declaring success. Used as the recovery flow when the autopilot reports calibration-required on arm, or when an operator triggers a 'fix calibration' from a system that's reporting `calibrationDeclined`. Composition rule v1 caps depth at 1 level, so every step here invokes an atomic (command-only) child recipe — no recipe-of-recipe-of-recipe. The runner synthesises the child's binary outcome down to a single FleetCommandResponse (success or error.unknown) which the matchers below branch on. Failing the parent on any child failure is intentional: a partial calibration sweep is worse than no sweep because the autopilot's pre-flight state is undefined.
Overall budget is 600s — right at the parser cap. Component budgets sum to ~560s (compass 300 + accel 180 + gyro 60 + armprobe 20) plus per-step setup overhead.

then.detail: Compass calibration failed — see audit trace for the failing child step. Recovery cannot continue without a converged compass.
Accelerometer calibration failed — see audit trace for the failing child step. Recovery cannot continue without a converged accel six-position pass.
Gyro calibration failed — see audit trace for the failing child step. Recovery cannot continue without a converged gyro null-rate pass.

All three calibrations converged but the post-cal arm probe still failed. The autopilot is reporting a non-calibration arm reason — inspect the probe's failing matcher detail (GPS, battery, mode, etc.) and escalate accordingly.
